claim: US-Israeli forces struck Iran's B1 bridge near Karaj, killing at least 8 people and injuring dozens.
Israeli forces launched a wide-scale wave of strikes including a massive explosion in the Jack neighborhood of northern Tehran on April 3.
US President Donald Trump threatened strikes on Iranian bridges and electric power plants via Truth Social posts.
The IRGC claimed responsibility for 'Wave 91 of Operation True Promise 4,' firing cruise missiles at the USS Abraham Lincoln carrier and ballistic missiles at a US gathering near a UAE base.
Iranian state media claimed air defenses downed a US F-35 jet over central Iran, with one claim including a captured pilot.
US officials denied Iranian claims of F-35 losses and a captured pilot.
Iranian drones struck a Kuwait oil refinery, with firefighters controlling the resulting blazes and no injuries reported.
Iran deployed drone swarms targeting Gulf infrastructure, with analysts noting thousands of drones remain available despite strikes on missiles.
US President Donald Trump announced 'productive' and 'constructive' talks with Iran, postponing strikes on energy infrastructure for five days.
A 15-point US peace plan delivered via Pakistan to Iran proposes reopening the Strait of Hormuz, missile limits, sanctions relief, and nuclear rollback.
Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi is reviewing a US peace plan but insists Iran has no intent to negotiate.
Trump threatened continued bombing if diplomacy fails and floated seizing Kharg Island oil or enriched uranium.
Following Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei's death in a February 28 US-Israel strike, his son Mojtaba Khamenei was appointed successor by the Assembly of Experts.
President Masoud Pezeshkian faces fierce hardline backlash from IRGC chief Ahmad Vahidi over conditional war-end signals, leading to a political deadlock.
IRGC is assuming key state functions amid political deadlock between hardliners and the civilian government.
Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr, an ex-IRGC deputy, was appointed as Supreme National Security Council chief, filling the security gap left by Ali Larijani's assassination.
Iran released 20 cargo ships as a negotiation sign, according to Trump's claims, though disputed by experts.
Iran's economy shows structural collapse with currency distrust and monetary regime shift amid war damages.
Iranian state TV warned citizens against revealing officials hiding among civilians and labeled protesters 'enemy combatants.'
Recent protests in Iran marked the country's most serious civil unrest ever, according to historical assessment.
US plans to deploy 2,000 additional Marines by Friday, according to Trump.
Iran's new leader Mojtaba Khamenei and General Abolfazl Shekarchi issued threats to adversaries' security and tourist sites worldwide.
Parliament speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf denied US talks with Iran.
